quote_of_day.text:
i just buy ORS packets and real fruit juice these days to cope with the fucking sun
they're cheaper

had to buy some coconut earlier and the cost of a small fist sized one was 35

soon a McD burger will be cheaper than home food
we will become obese just like mutts in the west
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste debate moved into the board, bringing scattered showers of argument. Anon tossed around claims and counter-claims about caste origins and 'Yadav' identity.

A fog of misinformation rolled in over the afternoon, with Anon claiming the site belongs to Rakulcel and another insisting China spends millions on Indian propaganda.

The visibility was poor, with no clear resolution in sight.

A cold snap of personal beefs chilled the board in the evening, with Anon airing grievances about randi gobar at the gym and another taking a swipe at a Swiss person.

The mood was brisk, with little warmth or empathy on display.

articles.title: Anon debates caste origins, 'Yadav' identity
articles.body:
Anon started a thread with a one-word post: 'caste?'. Replies quickly filled the thread with claims and counter-claims about the origins and social status of various castes, particularly 'Yadav'.

Anon argued that Yadavs are not a real caste but rather a group of people who started calling themselves descendants of Krishna. Another anon countered that Yadavs, particularly Ahirs, have distinct origins and were mentioned separately in the Mahabharat.

A respondent claimed that Ahirs in Bihar took up the surname Yadav during 'sanskritzation', while those found in UP, Haryana, and Rajasthan are the original Yadavs. One anon asserted that Jadau Rajputs are the actual descendants of Krishna, citing 'Vajranabha' as their lineage.

A counter-claim emerged that Vajranabha was a Kshatriya descendant, implying a martial race, and that Yadavs, particularly Ahirs, have always been part of this tradition. Anon also pointed out that there are mentions of Rajputs and Ahirs separately in the Mahabharat.

The discussion turned heated with accusations of 'caste cope' and personal attacks. Anon called another poster a 'bhangi' and claimed they were trying to hide their low birth behind their varna.

A respondent shot back, labeling the original poster a 'low born' and suggesting they were trying to project their own inferiority onto others. Some anons shared observations about the physical characteristics and social status of Yadavs in different regions.

One poster noted that their Rajasthani Yadav friends were fair-skinned, tall, and in the general category, while average Bihari Yadavs they saw were involved in menial labor and appeared 'dysgenic'.

Another anon claimed that good-looking Yadav women are rare and often marry into higher castes. The thread also touched on the idea that some castes, like Marathas, had undergone 'Kshatriyasisation', but Yadavs were seen as distinct in their claims of descent.

Anon dismissed the idea of divine or legendary origins for any caste, calling them 'fancy myths of origin'.

articles.title: Anon Claims Site Belongs to Rakulcel
articles.body:
Anon started a thread claiming that the site belongs to rakulcel and pointed to the number of threads rakulcel made that day. Another anon responded by criticising someone for not understanding a particular dynamic, mentioning "troon" and "hookers" in a derogatory context.

This poster also made a comment about dominance and sexual acts. A different poster referenced another user, calling them a "troon" and making claims about their interests and actions. This poster also brought up 4chan and its openness about certain topics.

Anon advised a newcomer to lurk for two years before posting on imageboards like 4chan, suggesting it was for their own good.

Someone else expressed regret over being exposed to the internet and imageboards during their childhood, feeling that it had wasted 20 years of their life. There were also discussions about being an "oldfag" or a newcomer to imageboards.

One poster mentioned they had been browsing 4chan since 2010 but still felt like a newfag at times. Anon shared their own experiences of being on the internet since 2020 and still exploring it.

The thread also involved discussions about other users, including someone who allegedly liked little kids and had a history of criminal activity. Anon disputed or downplayed these claims.

Anon debated the meaning of "oldfag" and shared their own experiences of being on imageboards since 2013 or 2010.

articles.title: Anon Claims China Spends Millions on Indian Propaganda
articles.body:
Anon posted that China has spent millions on propaganda to destroy India's image, spreading bot-crafted videos and defaming the country. This effort is now being exposed on Twitter, the poster claimed.

Another anon questioned the blind faith in the nation, suggesting that if someone dislikes aspects of their own country, such as certain festivals or destructive practices, they might defend propaganda against it.

This poster implied that such behavior makes one appear uneducated. A respondent argued that India operates with limited resources and cannot afford to make mistakes like other countries.

They suggested that critics are clueless about the challenges India faces and the role of influential figures like Ambani and Adani.

One anon expressed frustration that some people defend another nation, China, and highlighted what they see as hypocrisy and bias in political discussions. This poster described China as a "subhuman nation" where people live in difficult conditions.

Another poster mentioned the average IQ in India and questioned whether private individuals are qualified to manage critical infrastructure like nuclear reactors. They suggested that India's population density and limited resources contribute to its challenges.

Anon also discussed the relationship between political parties and national loyalty, with some accusing supporters of certain parties of being overly defensive of China. Others criticized the influence of wealthy businessmen like Adani and Ambani.

The conversation also touched on China's alleged treatment of lower castes, with some posters sharing claims about the Chinese carrying lower-caste individuals in cages and shipping them back to villages. Others questioned these claims and requested evidence.

Throughout the discussion, various anons expressed dissatisfaction with both major political parties in India, criticizing their policies and behaviors.

articles.title: Anon claims randi gobar made fun of him at gym
articles.body:
Anon posted about a randi gobar allegedly making fun of him at the gym. He claimed she smiled at him while he was doing elevated pushups.

Another anon responded, suggesting the poster focus on his own self-confidence, citing his own experience of a girl smiling at him in the gym and assuming she was interested.

Anon also joked about the original poster's gym routine, calling him a "pajeet" with an injured rotator cuff. One respondent defended doing pushups as a warmup exercise.

articles.title: Anon Claims Swiss Person is Retarded
articles.body:
Anon started a thread saying "better than swiss". Another anon responded, questioning the intelligence of someone who might prefer Indian content over Swiss, suggesting they were "extremely retarded or extremely jobless".

Anon also mentioned that a "true anon" would only bully a dalit but not rape a Muslim. One respondent claimed the "swissfaggot" was retarded and suggested dissecting their views from various perspectives.

Anon disputed the "swissjeet"'s intelligence, calling them a "retard".
